title: "REST IN PEACE" - Killed by a rabbit

title_analysis.chosen_title_reasoning: This title grounds the streamer's chaotic mid-clip screaming by explicitly spoiling the comedic payoff (dying to a rabbit). It avoids the bottom-quintile trap of using vague, all-caps rage quotes (e.g., "FUCK YOU RABBIT"), instead using the specific, absurd detail ("REST IN PEACE") to set clear expectations. This perfectly mirrors the strategy in "WHERE DID HE GO - The Mortuary Assistant," where a quote pairs with concrete context to anchor the chaos.

candidates_considered.text: "REST IN PEACE" - Killed by a rabbit
candidates_considered.reasoning: Grounds the chaotic reaction by spoiling the payoff while utilizing the most absurd quote from the clip to build a curiosity gap.
Relies entirely on `all_caps_intensity` and `genuine_reaction_peak`, which the synthesis explicitly identifies as bottom-quintile anti-patterns when lacking context.
A bit too vague; misses the opportunity to use the specific 'rest in peace' quote to create a stronger, more specific curiosity gap.
Lacks contextual grounding. The channel synthesis notes that `in_medias_res_chaos` needs aggressive contextualization (like naming the killer) to succeed.

patterns_applied: title_spoils_payoff — Explicitly stating he gets killed by a rabbit sets up the joke's premise and expectation immediately.
quoted_dialogue_title — Using 'REST IN PEACE' highlights the absurd catalyst for the streamer's rage, acting as a hook.
patterns_avoided: all_caps_intensity — Avoided using full all-caps for the entire title to prevent it from feeling like manufactured, unearned excitement.
genuine_reaction_peak — Avoided titling solely based on the scream/rage (e.g., 'WHY AM I ALIVE'), as the synthesis shows raw reactions fail without editorial framing.

reference_comparisons.reference_title: WHERE DID HE GO - The Mortuary Assistant
reference_comparisons.takeaway: Borrowed the structure of pairing a capitalized quote with concrete context to ground a chaotic, panic-driven reaction.
Borrowed the concept of setting a clear expectation for a punishment/payoff so the viewer knows exactly why they are watching the streamer scream.
